title: Bump cirrus image with easier dependency management
body:
Related:
https://github.com/containers/automation_images/issues/159
https://github.com/containers/automation_images/issues/161

Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: guess i need to tell CI to not download but install deps. Looking ..
Let's wait until buildah and skopeo also pass with the same change.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

> @lsm5 @cevich is this still being worked?

it is
Friendly ping.  Is this PR still needed?
Just rebased this. I think this can be merged once green, but let's wait for @cevich to confirm.

Dang, you'll have to remind me why this PR is bypassing `$PACKAGE_DOWNLOAD_DIR` to install packages at runtime.  Looking in c/automation_images, I see these packages are still being downloaded/saved into that directory.  Do you remember the reason we decided runtime-installs were a good idea here?

[Found it](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/issues/161)

> In cache_images/fedora_packaging script. It's installed via dependency resolution now, no need to download/cache it.

So likely the runtime install is a *noop*.  Which maybe begs the question, why bother with the `dnf` commands then?  Maybe it's worth removing them, since it will serve as a check on the image-build - ensuring the packages are indeed installed by dependency (as they should be).

> Dang, you'll have to remind me why this PR is bypassing `$PACKAGE_DOWNLOAD_DIR` to install packages at runtime. Looking in c/automation_images, I see these packages are still being downloaded/saved into that directory. Do you remember the reason we decided runtime-installs were a good idea here?

IIRC, the idea was to avoid downloading packages and fetch them from fedora's own repos.
@n1hility mind if I bother you about the Windows Smoke Test? It's failed quite a few times now.

@lsm5 sure anytime. It looks like https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/177 needs to be rebased to pick up the windows images changes, and the new image update in this PR pulled in. Then it should work

Aye, I rebased @lsm5 PR yesterday late.  That change hasn't made it here yet.  @vrothberg this failure is entirely expected.  Things get complicated when there are multiple CI VM Image build PRs running in parallel.  Not much we can do to simplify that.
I restarted the `bud podman fedora-37 root host` task.  Let's get this merged :smiley:
